description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rough, weathered surface and a mix of light gray, white, and brown tones. The rock displays patches of white mineral deposits and some darker, earth-stained areas, suggesting exposure to the elements. Its surface is uneven, with both smoother and more jagged sections, and it appears solid and dense. The rock's coloration and texture indicate it could be a type of sedimentary or weathered igneous stone, commonly found in outdoor urban environments. There are no visible carvings, graffiti, or artificial modifications on its surface. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or landscaping element in parks or along sidewalks.
description: A rough, irregularly shaped rock with gray, white, and brown tones, featuring weathered textures and mineral patches.
description_view_0: This view shows a broad, mostly gray face with white mineral speckles and a slightly sloped top.
description_view_1: This side reveals a more brownish, earth-stained surface with less visible mineral deposits and a rounded edge.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a dark, jagged section with a prominent flat gray face and white speckling.
description_view_3: This view presents a mostly brown, rough surface with subtle textural variations and a more vertical profile.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
